evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts` has four builder functions that mostly copy the same shared fields (`isVideo`, `readOnly`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `selectedProjectId`, `isCloudMode`) into new objects with minimal translation.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` builds a very large param bag for `useImageEditValue`, and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` re-splits and re-merges that bag into partial builders, adding indirection without reducing surface area.

`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` and `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` each define near-identical `TaskDbRow` shapes and snake_case-to-camelCase mappers (`mapDbTaskToTask` vs `mapDbTaskRowToTask`), indicating duplicate abstraction layers for the same contract.

`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx` repeatedly resolves the same values from multiple nested payload sources (`individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `parsedParams`), coupling UI rendering to historical payload layering from task/orchestrator builders.

`useImageEditValue` hardcodes `isFlippedHorizontally: false` and `isSaving: false` in the returned `ImageEditState`, so the returned contract can diverge from real UI state (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:183-184`).

Downstream lightbox/edit components consume the shared image edit state object as authoritative (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx`), increasing risk of stale or misleading flags.

`createGenerationFromTask` accepts `taskData: unknown` but immediately dereferences `taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, and `taskData.task_type` without narrowing (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:42,47,50,122`).
`calculate-task-cost` force-casts DB results via `task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null`, bypassing structural checks before nested access (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:227`).
`useButtonGroupProps` memoizes with `[params]`, so memoization is invalidated whenever the wrapper object identity changes (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts:142`).

`useImageEditValue` repeats the same pattern with `useMemo(..., [params])`, making the memo mostly ceremonial and obscuring real dependency intent (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:237-239`).
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is named as a broad utility hub but only re-exports `cn`, `cropFilename`, and `truncateText` (`src/shared/lib/utils.ts:1-2`).

High-traffic UI modules import from this generic name (`src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`), which hides actual domain intent (class name composition vs string formatting).
naming_quality.unreported_risk: Given import breadth, this generic entrypoint can keep attracting unrelated helpers, increasing semantic drift and making dependency intent harder to trace.

evidence: In `complete_task`, DB failures in orchestrator paths are often logged then converted to `null`/early return (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:214-215,239`), which suppresses upstream failure signaling.
Sibling generation path logs and rethrows (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:138-143`), creating mixed failure contracts in the same completion subsystem.

findings.summary: Button-group prop builders add repeated pass-through indirection with little behavior.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` builds `topLeft/topRight/bottomLeft/bottomRight` via four separate builders that each re-copy the same shared fields.
`LightboxLayout` consumes these shaped props directly via spread (`<BottomLeftControls {...props.buttonGroupProps.bottomLeft} />`, etc.), so the extra layer mostly re-packages existing data.
Render-model hooks already perform additional prop assembly, creating stacked wrappers for the same control inputs.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to a single typed base object plus small per-group deltas (or pass shared context directly to controls) and remove the four near-duplicate builder functions.

findings.summary: Inline edit state composition uses oversized param bags and split/merge wrappers.
evidence: `buildImageEditValueParams` in `useInlineEditState` forwards a very large object of fields/callbacks with minimal transformation.
`useImageEditValue` then re-partitions the same bag into multiple `build*State` helpers and merges them back into one `ImageEditState`.
`InlineEditView` consumes only slices of that assembled object, so the extra assembly layers increase wiring complexity more than reuse.
findings.suggestion: Replace the monolithic `UseImageEditValueParams` with smaller domain slices (mode/canvas/reposition/form) and expose those slices directly to consumers; keep one canonical source per slice instead of split-then-merge builders.

findings.summary: Task DB-to-domain mapping is duplicated across hooks and bridge utilities.
evidence: Both files define a `TaskDbRow` interface with the same snake_case columns and similar optionality.
Both files implement near-identical mappers (`mapDbTaskToTask` and `mapDbTaskRowToTask`) performing the same field renaming and defaulting logic.
This creates parallel abstractions for one contract, increasing drift risk when task schema evolves.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared mapper module (`taskRowMapper`) used by both `useTasks` and `generationTaskBridge`, and delete one duplicate interface/mapping implementation.

findings.summary: UI details logic is tightly coupled to multiple historical payload layers.
evidence: `VideoTravelDetails` resolves many fields by scanning several containers in order (`individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `parsedParams`).
`payloadBuilder` and completion/generation routing preserve multiple payload shapes and compatibility paths, so the UI must understand orchestration internals instead of a normalized view model.
The repeated fallback chains in details rendering are a sign of wide-bag API leakage across boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a normalized task-details adapter (single schema) at the boundary where task params are parsed, and make `VideoTravelDetails` consume only that normalized shape.

findings.summary: Task pipeline uses `unknown` and double-casts that bypass runtime shape guarantees.
evidence: `taskData` is typed `unknown` but dereferenced directly (`taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, `taskData.task_type`) in `createGenerationFromTask`.
`const typedTask = task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null` suppresses structural validation before nested property reads.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit runtime-validated task DTOs (e.g., Zod/manual guards) and narrow before access. Replace `unknown` + forced casts with parsed types (`ParsedTaskData`, `TaskWithProject`) returned from validation helpers.

findings.summary: Completion flow mixes silent returns and thrown errors for similar DB failures.
evidence: Orchestrator branch logs query errors and returns `null`/early (`Error querying segments`, `Error checking final stitch status`).
Generation branch logs then rethrows in catch, causing caller-visible failure for equivalent persistence issues.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one contract for this subsystem: either propagate typed errors upward and handle at boundary, or consistently return structured result objects (`{ok:false, reason,...}`) and avoid mixed semantics.

findings.summary: Memoized builders depend on whole params object, defeating memo intent.
evidence: Both hooks use `useMemo(..., [params])`, so any recreated wrapper object forces recomputation.
This adds indirection without stable dependency semantics, obscuring when recomputation is actually expected.
findings.suggestion: Either remove `useMemo` entirely or depend on explicit primitive/function fields (or pre-memoize `params` upstream) so recomputation behavior is intentional and reviewable.

findings.summary: Returned `ImageEditState` advertises live flags that are hardcoded constants.
evidence: `buildRepositionAndPanelState` sets `isFlippedHorizontally: false` and `isSaving: false` unconditionally.
Layout consumers treat the shared image edit object as the unified state source, so static placeholders can drift from behavior as features evolve.
findings.suggestion: Populate these fields from real source hooks (flip/save state) or remove/deprecate them from `ImageEditState` until they are truthfully implemented.

findings.summary: `shared/lib/utils.ts` is overly generic for its narrow exports and usage.
evidence: The file name implies broad catch-all utilities but currently re-exports only class-name/string helpers.
Core UI modules import `cn` through this generic path, masking the true dependency domain and encouraging future utility accretion.
findings.suggestion: Rename/split to intent-specific entrypoints (for example `uiClassNames.ts` direct import path and `stringFormatting.ts` barrel) and migrate callers to explicit modules.
